title: Implement schema validation and message envelope
purpose: Implement the schema type system and message envelope. Schemas define the expected message structure for a channel. Publish-time validation rejects non-conforming messages before any subscriber sees them (ADR-002). The envelope carries payload, causal context, schema identifier, publisher identity, and timestamp as the system-wide unit of routing, persistence, and delivery.
task: Replace the SchemaRef placeholder from LIM-001 with a real schema definition supporting JSON Schema validation. Implement publish-time validation in the channel actor's publish path. Define the message envelope with all required fields. Implement schema evolution (add field with default) without requiring subscriber redeployment. The schema is owned by the channel — no external schema registry.

requirements.title: Define Schema type for channel message validation
requirements.spec: A Schema type SHALL define the expected message structure for a channel. It SHALL support JSON Schema as the validation format. Schema SHALL be Clone + Send + Sync for sharing across the system.
acceptance: Schema type defined in crates/liminal/src/channel/schema.rs
Schema wraps a JSON Schema definition (serde_json::Value or a parsed representation)
Schema provides a validate(payload) -> Result<(), SchemaValidationError> method
Schema is Clone + Send + Sync

files: crates/liminal/src/channel/schema.rs:1-1 — empty target file; the Schema type, SchemaValidationError, validation helpers, and likely SchemaId belong here.
crates/liminal/src/channel/types.rs:13-33 — current `SchemaRef` is an opaque unit placeholder used by `ChannelConfig`; replacing/filling it is the API integration point.
crates/liminal/src/channel/mod.rs:1-5 — currently only declares/re-exports `subscription` and `types`; add `pub mod schema` and re-export schema types here, with no logic in mod.rs.
crates/liminal/Cargo.toml:11-17 — direct dependencies currently omit `serde_json` and any JSON Schema validator; transitive `serde_json` from beamr is not enough for crate code.
Cargo.toml:14-23 — workspace lints deny unsafe, clippy all/pedantic, unwrap, expect, and panic, so schema code and tests should use explicit Result propagation.
context: `SchemaRef` is still a LIM-001 placeholder; the brief task says to replace it with the real channel-owned schema definition, but preserving a compatibility alias/re-export may reduce downstream churn.
All public types need `Debug`; `SchemaValidationError` should derive/implement `std::error::Error` via thiserror just like `LiminalError` and `ProtocolError`.

If using a parsed JSON Schema validator, verify it is `Send + Sync`; the safest shape is `Schema { definition: serde_json::Value, validator: Arc<...>, id: SchemaId, ... }` so `Schema` can be cloned cheaply.

`ChannelConfig` currently derives `PartialEq, Eq`; a real compiled validator may not support those derives, so either equality must be based on a comparable schema representation/id or the derive set on `ChannelConfig` needs revisiting deliberately.
scout.approach: Add direct manifest dependencies for JSON handling/validation, then implement `Schema` in `channel/schema.rs` around a JSON Schema definition plus any compiled validator state. Provide constructors that validate/compile the schema definition without panics, a `validate(payload: impl AsRef<[u8]>) -> Result<(), SchemaValidationError>` path that parses payload bytes as JSON and validates against the schema, and a `SchemaId` newtype or accessor for the envelope work. Update `channel/types.rs` so `ChannelConfig` owns the real `Schema` (or a clearly named schema handle/reference to it), and update `channel/mod.rs` to declare/re-export `schema::{Schema, SchemaId, SchemaValidationError}` while keeping mod.rs declaration-only.
scout.notes: The file list in the brief does not mention `Cargo.toml`, but R1 is not implementable cleanly without direct `serde_json` and validator dependencies. Do not rely on JSON Schema `default` behavior here; defaults are annotations and R3 needs explicit payload normalization.

files_changed.note: Implemented Schema, SchemaId, SchemaValidationError, JSON Schema compilation/validation, default normalization support, and schema-focused tests.
Declared actor/schema modules and re-exported Schema, SchemaId, SchemaValidationError while keeping mod.rs declaration/re-export only.
Replaced the placeholder schema field with real Schema ownership and retained SchemaRef as a compatibility alias.
Added direct serde_json, jsonschema, chrono, and uuid dependencies needed by the core schema/envelope model.
Updated lockfile for newly added direct dependencies.

dev.how: Schema now wraps a JSON Schema definition plus a compiled jsonschema::Validator behind Arc for cheap Clone sharing and Send/Sync use. It exposes validate(payload) -> Result<(), SchemaValidationError>, explicit SchemaId support, and an error type implementing std::error::Error; ChannelConfig owns the concrete Schema while SchemaRef remains an alias for downstream compatibility.

checklist.note: Schema defines the channel message contract and validates JSON payload bytes against the wrapped JSON Schema.

stories.note: Application developers can attach a schema to the channel contract and get runtime validation before subscribers see data.

acceptance.criterion: Schema type defined in crates/liminal/src/channel/schema.rs
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/channel/schema.rs:38-45 defines public Schema; crates/liminal/src/channel/mod.rs:1-8 declares and re-exports the schema module/types.

acceptance.criterion: Schema wraps a JSON Schema definition (serde_json::Value or a parsed representation)
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/channel/schema.rs:41-44 stores definition: Arc<Value> and validator: Arc<jsonschema::Validator>; construction compiles the JSON Schema at schema.rs:145-161.

acceptance.criterion: Schema provides a validate(payload) -> Result<(), SchemaValidationError> method
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/channel/schema.rs:79-91 implements pub fn validate<Payload>(&self, payload) -> Result<(), SchemaValidationError>; test channel::schema::tests::validates_payload_against_json_schema at schema.rs:340-352 covers accept/reject behavior.

acceptance.criterion: Schema is Clone + Send + Sync
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/channel/schema.rs:38-45 derives Clone and stores Arc-backed fields; test channel::schema::tests::schema_is_clone_send_sync at schema.rs:333-338 compile-asserts Clone + Send + Sync + Debug.

requirements.title: Implement publish-time schema validation
requirements.spec: WHEN a message is published to a channel, the channel actor SHALL validate the message payload against the channel's declared schema BEFORE delivering to any subscriber. IF validation fails, THEN the publish SHALL return LiminalError::SchemaMismatch and SHALL NOT deliver the message to any subscriber.
acceptance: Channel actor calls schema.validate(payload) before fan-out
SchemaMismatch error is returned to the publisher on validation failure
No subscriber receives a message that failed validation
Test: publish valid message → delivered; publish invalid message → SchemaMismatch, no delivery

files: crates/liminal/src/channel/actor.rs:1-1 — empty target; there is no existing fan-out path to insert validation into.

crates/liminal/src/channel/types.rs:36-51 — `ChannelHandle::publish` currently drops the payload and returns `Ok(())`, so publisher-visible `SchemaMismatch` cannot happen until the handle is wired to actor logic.
crates/liminal/src/channel/subscription.rs:1-8 — subscription handle is only a stub; tests for “no subscriber receives invalid message” will need a minimal observable delivery mechanism.
crates/liminal/src/error.rs:12-18 — `LiminalError::SchemaMismatch { message: String }` already exists and should be the publisher-facing error on validation failure.
docs/design/core/briefs/LIM-002.json:30-43 — prior design describes the intended actor commands/fan-out baseline, but this branch has not implemented it.
context: The design requires the channel actor to be the single authority for subscriber list, schema, and lifecycle; validation belongs inside actor publish handling, not in subscribers or downstream routing.
Validation must happen before any delivery/fan-out loop and before constructing/persisting/routing side effects; the invalid path should return immediately with `LiminalError::SchemaMismatch`.

Because `actor.rs` is empty, this requirement may require a small actor/state abstraction for tests even if full beamr supervision from LIM-002 is absent; avoid implementing routing, persistence, or wire-protocol behavior to compensate.
The liminal manifest now has `haematite`, but CN7 still means the ephemeral channel publish path must not call or serialize for haematite.
scout.approach: Define the channel actor publish path so it owns a `Schema` and subscriber list, accepts payload bytes, calls `schema.validate(&payload)` first, maps `SchemaValidationError` to `LiminalError::SchemaMismatch { message }`, and returns without touching subscribers on failure. On success, pass the validated payload into R4 envelope construction and deliver cloned `Envelope`s to the current subscribers. If the full beamr actor baseline is still absent, implement the smallest internal actor/state API needed to make this ordering testable from `ChannelHandle::publish`, but keep all persistence, routing, and crash-supervision additions out of scope for this brief.
scout.notes: Tests should prove absence of delivery, not just presence of an error: subscribe/attach a test receiver, publish an invalid JSON payload, assert the result matches `SchemaMismatch`, then assert the receiver queue remains empty. Use `Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>>` tests with `?` instead of unwrap/expect to satisfy the workspace clippy configuration.

files_changed.note: Implemented minimal channel actor state with schema-owned validation before envelope fan-out and tests for valid/invalid publish behavior.
Wired ChannelHandle::publish/subscribe/close to actor state so SchemaMismatch reaches publishers.
Replaced the subscription stub with an in-memory envelope inbox so tests and callers can observe delivered validated messages.

dev.how: The channel actor now validates payloads before any subscriber delivery, maps SchemaValidationError to LiminalError::SchemaMismatch, returns immediately on invalid input, and only constructs/fans out Envelopes for validated payloads. Tests cover valid delivery and invalid publish returning SchemaMismatch while leaving the subscriber queue empty.

checklist.note: Invalid schema payloads are rejected with LiminalError::SchemaMismatch.
Validation happens before envelope creation and before the delivery loop touches subscribers.

stories.note: Subscribers never receive messages that fail the channel schema in the tested publish path.

acceptance.criterion: Channel actor calls schema.validate(payload) before fan-out
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/channel/actor.rs:47-74 performs schema.validate(payload) at lines 60-62 before Envelope::new at lines 67-72 and deliver at line 73.

acceptance.criterion: SchemaMismatch error is returned to the publisher on validation failure
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/channel/actor.rs:60-66 maps SchemaValidationError through schema_mismatch; schema_mismatch returns LiminalError::SchemaMismatch at actor.rs:114-118. Public publish path routes publisher calls to actor.publish at crates/liminal/src/channel/types.rs:72-110.

acceptance.criterion: No subscriber receives a message that failed validation
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/channel/actor.rs:60-66 returns on validation/defaulting error before Envelope construction and deliver; test channel::actor::tests::publish_invalid_message_returns_schema_mismatch_without_delivery asserts SchemaMismatch and empty subscription at actor.rs:151-161.

acceptance.criterion: Test: publish valid message → delivered; publish invalid message → SchemaMismatch, no delivery
acceptance.evidence: Test channel::actor::tests::publish_valid_message_delivers_envelope at crates/liminal/src/channel/actor.rs:134-149 covers valid delivery; channel::actor::tests::publish_invalid_message_returns_schema_mismatch_without_delivery at actor.rs:151-161 covers invalid SchemaMismatch and no delivery.

requirements.title: Implement schema evolution support
requirements.spec: Schema evolution (adding a field with a default value) SHALL be supported without subscriber redeployment. WHEN a schema is evolved, new messages validate against the new schema. Existing subscribers continue receiving messages — the new field appears with its default for consumers that don't know about it.
acceptance: Schema supports an evolve operation that adds a field with a default
Messages published after evolution include the new field
Existing subscribers are not disconnected or disrupted by evolution
Test: evolve schema → publish with new field → old subscriber still receives

files: crates/liminal/src/channel/schema.rs:1-1 — evolution logic and default application belong beside validation so one schema object owns both contract and normalization.
crates/liminal/src/channel/types.rs:13-33 — `ChannelConfig` carries the channel schema; channel creation should not hide schema evolution behind defaults or an external registry.
crates/liminal/src/channel/actor.rs:1-1 — actor state will need to swap/evolve its owned schema without rebuilding or disconnecting subscribers.
docs/design/core/design.json:6-6 — design states schema evolution is channel-level and the channel holds its own schema registry, not an external service.
docs/design/core/briefs/LIM-003.json:50-62 — acceptance specifically requires add-field-with-default behavior and uninterrupted existing subscribers.
context: JSON Schema `default` does not mutate instances during validation; to make “new field appears with its default” true, the implementation must explicitly insert defaults into object payloads before delivery.

The R1 `validate(payload) -> Result<(), ...>` signature only reports validity; R3 likely needs an additional internal method such as `validate_and_apply_defaults(Vec<u8>) -> Result<Vec<u8>, ...>` or `normalize_value(&mut Value)` for the actor delivery path.

Evolving the schema should be a channel-owned operation that updates schema state/schema id while preserving subscriber registrations; do not model it as unsubscribe/resubscribe or a global schema registry.

Adding a required field with a default is safest if defaults are applied before validation against the evolved schema; otherwise old publishers that omit the new field may fail validation before default insertion.
scout.approach: Represent evolution metadata in `Schema` (for example, a map of field names to default `serde_json::Value`s and the evolved JSON Schema definition). Implement an `evolve_add_field(name, field_schema, default)` operation that returns a new `Schema` or updates actor-owned schema state, inserts the field schema under object `properties`, records the default, and recompiles/validates the schema definition. The actor publish path should parse payload into a JSON object, apply recorded defaults for missing fields, validate the normalized value against the current schema, then reserialize the normalized payload bytes into the envelope; subscribers remain in the same actor state throughout.
scout.notes: Handle non-object schemas/payloads explicitly: adding a named field only makes sense for object schemas, so return `SchemaValidationError` rather than panicking or silently doing nothing. Also decide whether schema evolution changes `SchemaId`; R4 needs `schema_id` to identify the schema version that validated each message.

files_changed.note: Added evolve_add_field plus explicit default collection/application and validation of default values.
Added actor-owned schema evolution that swaps schema versions without modifying subscriber registrations.
Exposed ChannelHandle schema evolution and current schema-id helpers.

dev.how: Schema evolution adds a field schema under object properties, records the provided default, makes the field required, recompiles the JSON Schema, and generates a new SchemaId. The actor swaps only its owned Schema, preserving subscribers; publish normalization inserts defaults before validation and envelope construction so delivered payloads include the evolved field.

checklist.note: Adding a defaulted field is supported and tested through both Schema and actor/handle publish flows.

acceptance.criterion: Schema supports an evolve operation that adds a field with a default
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/channel/schema.rs:111-143 implements Schema::evolve_add_field; it validates the default at schema.rs:127 and schema.rs:256-267, inserts the property/default at schema.rs:284-306, adds it to required at schema.rs:308-326, and recompiles via from_parts at schema.rs:142-161.

acceptance.criterion: Messages published after evolution include the new field
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/channel/schema.rs:99-108 returns normalized delivery bytes after validation; defaults are applied at schema.rs:174-187. Test channel::actor::tests::evolved_schema_keeps_existing_subscriber_and_applies_default asserts delivered payload priority default at crates/liminal/src/channel/actor.rs:164-181; hardened test actor_evolution_does_not_disconnect_existing_subscribers publishes explicit priority and asserts it at actor.rs:193-203.

acceptance.criterion: Existing subscribers are not disconnected or disrupted by evolution
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/channel/actor.rs:76-87 evolves by replacing only self.schema and leaves subscribers untouched; test channel::actor::tests::actor_evolution_does_not_disconnect_existing_subscribers subscribes before evolution and receives after evolution at actor.rs:185-203.

acceptance.criterion: Test: evolve schema → publish with new field → old subscriber still receives
acceptance.evidence: Hardened test channel::actor::tests::actor_evolution_does_not_disconnect_existing_subscribers at crates/liminal/src/channel/actor.rs:185-203 evolves schema, publishes a payload containing priority, receives via the pre-existing subscriber, and asserts the evolved field is present.

issues: The original evolution subscriber test proved default insertion but did not explicitly publish a payload containing the newly evolved field, leaving the acceptance test wording under-covered.
fixes: Updated channel::actor::tests::actor_evolution_does_not_disconnect_existing_subscribers to publish {order_id, quantity, priority} after evolution and assert the old subscriber receives priority == "urgent" at crates/liminal/src/channel/actor.rs:193-203.

requirements.title: Define message envelope
requirements.spec: Every message SHALL travel in an Envelope containing: payload (bytes), causal_context (optional parent reference), schema_id (which schema validated this message), publisher_id (identity of the publisher), and timestamp (when the message was published). The Envelope SHALL be the unit of routing, persistence, and delivery throughout the system.
acceptance: Envelope struct defined in crates/liminal/src/envelope.rs
Envelope has fields: payload (Bytes or Vec<u8>), causal_context (Option<CausalContext>), schema_id (SchemaId), publisher_id (PublisherId), timestamp (DateTime<Utc>)
Envelope derives Clone, Debug
Envelope is used as the delivery unit in the channel actor (replacing raw payload)

files: crates/liminal/src/envelope.rs:1-1 — empty target for the core `Envelope` and likely `PublisherId` newtype.
crates/liminal/src/causal.rs:1-1 — empty target for `CausalContext`/`MessageId`, which `Envelope` must reference as `Option<CausalContext>`.
crates/liminal/src/channel/actor.rs:1-1 — actor delivery unit must become `Envelope`, not raw payload bytes.

crates/liminal/src/protocol/frame.rs:189-200 — protocol publish frames currently carry opaque `Vec<u8>` and a numeric ack id; this is wire shape, not the core envelope serialization to implement here.
crates/liminal/Cargo.toml:11-17 — `DateTime<Utc>` requires a direct chrono dependency; `Vec<u8>` avoids needing a direct bytes dependency unless the implementer chooses `Bytes`.
context: The core `Envelope` should derive `Clone, Debug` and contain exactly the requested fields: payload bytes, optional causal context, schema id, publisher id, and publish timestamp.

No existing `SchemaId`, `PublisherId`, or timestamp dependency exists in the core modules; use small public newtypes with `Debug`/clone/equality derives rather than unstructured bare strings where possible.
Timestamp should represent publish time, so the channel actor should set it when accepting a publish; a `with_timestamp` constructor is useful for deterministic tests.
Do not fill `protocol/envelope.rs` or encode/decode the envelope in this brief; protocol/wire serialization is explicitly a later cluster.
scout.approach: Define `Envelope` in `crates/liminal/src/envelope.rs` using `Vec<u8>` for payload, `Option<CausalContext>` from R5, `SchemaId` from the schema module, a new `PublisherId` type, and `chrono::DateTime<chrono::Utc>` for timestamp. Provide small constructors/accessors that keep fields inspectable for tests and avoid panics. Then change the channel actor’s successful publish path to construct an `Envelope` only after schema validation/default normalization and fan out that envelope to subscribers; leave persistence and routing as future consumers of the same envelope type rather than implementing them now.
scout.notes: If choosing `bytes::Bytes` instead of `Vec<u8>`, add it as a direct dependency despite its current transitive presence in `Cargo.lock`. For chrono, ensure the selected features support `Utc::now()` if the actor constructor sets the timestamp internally.

files_changed.note: Implemented Envelope and PublisherId with payload, causal context, schema id, publisher id, and DateTime<Utc> timestamp.
Successful publishes now deliver Envelope instances instead of raw payload bytes.
Subscriptions store and return Envelope values as the delivery unit.

dev.how: Envelope is a Clone + Debug core type using Vec<u8> payloads, Option<CausalContext>, SchemaId, PublisherId, and chrono DateTime<Utc>. Channel actor publish constructs an Envelope only after validation/default normalization and fans cloned Envelopes to subscribers, leaving routing, persistence, and wire serialization untouched.

checklist.note: Envelope carries payload, causal context, schema identifier, publisher identity, and timestamp.
The implemented channel delivery path uses Envelope as the subscriber delivery unit.

acceptance.criterion: Envelope struct defined in crates/liminal/src/envelope.rs
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/envelope.rs:42-55 defines public Envelope.

acceptance.criterion: Envelope has fields: payload (Bytes or Vec<u8>), causal_context (Option<CausalContext>), schema_id (SchemaId), publisher_id (PublisherId), timestamp (DateTime<Utc>)
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/envelope.rs:45-54 contains payload: Vec<u8>, causal_context: Option<CausalContext>, schema_id: SchemaId, publisher_id: PublisherId, and timestamp: DateTime<Utc>; test envelope::tests::envelope_carries_required_fields asserts all at envelope.rs:96-117.

acceptance.criterion: Envelope derives Clone, Debug
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/envelope.rs:42-44 derives Clone and Debug for Envelope.

acceptance.criterion: Envelope is used as the delivery unit in the channel actor (replacing raw payload)
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/channel/actor.rs:67-73 constructs an Envelope after validation and passes it to deliver; actor.rs:95-110 fans cloned Envelope values to subscribers; crates/liminal/src/channel/subscription.rs:7-13 stores VecDeque<Envelope> and try_next returns Option<Envelope> at subscription.rs:27-40.

requirements.title: Define causal context for per-chain ordering
requirements.spec: CausalContext SHALL contain an optional parent message reference (MessageId) enabling per-causal-chain ordering (ADR-005). Messages with a parent reference are ordered after their parent. Independent messages (no parent) can be delivered in parallel.
acceptance: CausalContext struct defined in crates/liminal/src/causal.rs
CausalContext has parent: Option<MessageId> field
MessageId is a unique identifier (UUID or equivalent)
CausalContext derives Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq

files: crates/liminal/src/causal.rs:1-1 — empty target for `CausalContext` and `MessageId`.
crates/liminal/src/envelope.rs:1-1 — envelope must embed `Option<CausalContext>` but should not enforce ordering itself.
docs/design/core/briefs/LIM-003.json:86-99 — this brief’s acceptance limits R5 to the data model: parent reference and unique message id.
docs/design/core/briefs/LIM-006.json:10-11 — later brief will add ordering utilities/orderer on top of this data model; do not implement that now.
crates/liminal/Cargo.toml:11-17 — no direct uuid dependency exists; add one if `MessageId` is UUID-backed.
context: R5 is data-model only: define `CausalContext { parent: Option<MessageId> }` and `MessageId`, but do not buffer, order, route, or time out messages.
`CausalContext` must derive `Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq`; make `MessageId` derive at least `Clone, Copy` if UUID-backed, `Debug`, `PartialEq`, `Eq`, and likely `Hash` for future orderer use.
A UUID-backed `MessageId` is the most direct way to satisfy uniqueness; the repo currently has `rand` but no uuid crate, and rolling a custom id generator risks more code and uniqueness edge cases.
Independent messages are represented by `parent: None`; parented messages merely carry the reference in this brief, with ordering enforcement deferred to routing/LIM-006.
scout.approach: Add a small `MessageId` newtype around `uuid::Uuid` with a `new()`/`generate()` constructor plus accessors/from-uuid helpers for tests, and define `CausalContext` with a public or accessor-backed `parent: Option<MessageId>` field. Add convenience constructors such as `CausalContext::root()` and `CausalContext::child_of(parent)` if they do not obscure the required field shape. Wire `Envelope` to use `Option<CausalContext>` and optionally re-export `CausalContext`/`MessageId` from `lib.rs` or rely on the existing public `causal` module, but do not create `causal/orderer.rs` or enforce happened-before delivery here.
scout.notes: Avoid `SystemTime` for envelope timestamps; R4 explicitly wants `DateTime<Utc>`. If uuid v4 generation is used, ensure the crate feature is enabled and tests avoid unwrap/expect.

files_changed.note: Implemented UUID-backed MessageId and CausalContext with optional parent field plus data-model tests.
Added direct uuid dependency with v4 generation for unique MessageId and SchemaId values.

dev.how: CausalContext is a Clone + Debug + PartialEq + Eq data model with a public parent: Option<MessageId> field. MessageId is a UUID-backed Copy/Clone/Debug/Eq/Hash newtype with generation and from/as UUID helpers; no causal ordering enforcement, buffering, routing, or timeouts were added.

checklist.note: Causal context contains the optional parent message reference needed for later per-chain ordering work.

acceptance.criterion: CausalContext struct defined in crates/liminal/src/causal.rs
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/causal.rs:33-38 defines public CausalContext.

acceptance.criterion: CausalContext has parent: Option<MessageId> field
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/causal.rs:35-38 contains pub parent: Option<MessageId>; tests root_context_has_no_parent and child_context_carries_parent_reference assert both forms at causal.rs:66-77.

acceptance.criterion: MessageId is a unique identifier (UUID or equivalent)
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/causal.rs:1-5 backs MessageId with uuid::Uuid; MessageId::new uses Uuid::new_v4() at causal.rs:7-12; generated_message_ids_are_unique test covers distinct IDs at causal.rs:79-85.

acceptance.criterion: CausalContext derives Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/causal.rs:33-35 derives Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq for CausalContext.

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement the routing engine — that is the routing cluster.
SHALL NOT implement persistence of envelopes — that is the durability cluster.
SHALL NOT implement wire serialisation of envelopes — that is the protocol cluster.
SHALL NOT implement causal ordering enforcement — that is a routing concern. This brief defines the data model only.
verification: cargo clippy -p liminal -- -D warnings passes clean.
Publishing an invalid message returns SchemaMismatch and delivers nothing.
Envelope is used as the delivery unit throughout the channel actor.
CausalContext is present in the envelope with optional parent reference.
